text.title: OpenAI Function Calling — Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 production failure modes when using tool use or structured outputs. Each carries symptom / root-cause / fix.

failure-mode.description: Tool result appended before the assistant message that produced the call — API rejects the request with 400.
failure-mode.detector: BadRequestError with "tool message must follow assistant message with tool_calls"; downstream tool result count != preceding assistant tool_call count.
failure-mode.repair: Always: append assistant message first, then iterate `tool_calls`, append each `role:tool` result keyed by `tool_call_id`. Wrap in a helper to enforce order.

failure-mode.description: `message.parsed` accessed without checking `message.refusal`; AttributeError when refusal triggered.
failure-mode.detector: Production traceback `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute ...` from a `parsed.X` access.
failure-mode.repair: Always: `if msg.refusal: raise RefusalError(msg.refusal); person = msg.parsed`. Add a unit test that mocks a refusal response.

failure-mode.description: Parallel tool calls execute contradictory writes (e.g., create + delete on the same record), corrupting state.
failure-mode.detector: Audit log shows opposite-direction writes within the same model turn; downstream consistency check fails.
failure-mode.repair: Run `coherence_check(tool_calls)` before dispatch: detect (create, delete), (debit, credit), (lock, unlock) on the same id; if found, surface to the model for clarification instead of executing.

failure-mode.description: `parse` invoked on an old model (gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-4-turbo); NotImplementedError at runtime.
failure-mode.detector: Stack trace `NotImplementedError: response_format is not supported by this model`.
failure-mode.repair: Capability-gate on the model id (allowlist gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1, o3-mini); fall back to `json_object` + manual Pydantic validation for unsupported models.

failure-mode.description: Tool dispatched via `eval(function.name)(...)` or dynamic import — arbitrary code execution via a poisoned tool schema.
failure-mode.detector: Code review finds `eval` or `__import__` in dispatch path; security scan flags dynamic call on model output.
failure-mode.repair: Centralise dispatch in a `tool_registry: dict[str, callable]` initialised at startup. Unknown name → KeyError raised, not silently looked up.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Tool arguments returned by the model are DATA. Treat them with the same skepticism as any other user input: validate types with Pydantic, reject out-of-range values, never inject directly into shell or SQL without parameterisation.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Any change to the tool registry (adding a new tool, changing a signature) goes through code review by a named engineer. New tools that touch payment, identity, or external write APIs additionally require a security review.
